chunk_summary: This case explores delayed texture progression in a child with neurodisability, focusing on caregiver fear, pain drivers, feeding skill mismatch, and structured texture advancement using IDDSI guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of pain, skill mismatch, and caregiver-child dynamics in feeding difficulties.
Learn how to use IDDSI texture ladders for structured feeding progression.
Identify triggers for referral to feeding therapists or SLPs in cases of persistent feeding challenges.
Recognize the importance of addressing caregiver fear to ensure safe and consistent feeding practices.
key_facts: The child is 26 months old with global developmental delay and eats only smooth purées.
Mealtimes last 60 minutes, and weight is falling across centiles.
Pain drivers like constipation or reflux should be screened and addressed first.
Use IDDSI texture ladders to advance feeding skills systematically, changing one variable at a time.
Referral to a feeding therapist or SLP is recommended if weight faltering persists or caregiver fear remains high.
reference_quote: Pediatric Feeding Disorder: Consensus Definition and Conceptual Framework emphasizes the importance of addressing pain, skill mismatch, and caregiver dynamics in feeding challenges. (Source: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30358739/)
